summary: Fiery second set with explosive Eyes of the World and Not Fade Away
blurb: The May‑7‑1979 Lafayette College show delivers a high‑octane second set highlighted by an epic Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World → Not Fade Away marathon. Jerry’s lead work is scorching, Brent adds fresh keyboard fire, and the Rhythm Devils lock in with generous drum passages. Recommended for fans of the early Brent era and the band’s late‑70s jam intensity.
review:
On May 7 1979 the Grateful Dead took the stage at Allan Kirby Field House in Easton, PA, just weeks into Brent Mydland’s debut with the crew. The first set rolls through a solid mix of roots numbers and ballads—Don’t Ease Me In, Candyman, Peggy‑O and Passenger—all played with tight group chemistry. The real fireworks erupt in the second set: Estimated Prophet launches a soaring jam that morphs into an explosive Eyes of the World, then erupts into a monster Not Fade Away featuring guest guitarist John Cipollina. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, his vocal power lifts every chorus, and his jazzy solo on Candyman still shines in the mix. Brent, only day 15 with the band, already shows his delicate touch on Peggy‑O and throws down energetic organ/synth textures that drive the second‑set momentum. Phil lays down a strong bass foundation while adding more extroverted vocal moments, and the Rhythm Devils—Mickey and Billy—deliver generous, double‑drummed dynamics throughout the jam corridor, culminating in a joyous Black Peter into Around & Around finish. The audience recording (gd79-05-07.glassberg.vernon.18877.sbeok.shnf) captures the live energy with clear auditorium ambience, making this show a must‑listen for anyone craving that late‑70s Dead fire.  

While a mixed‑source matrix version exists, the glassberg audience tape offers the best balance of clarity and atmosphere, preserving the explosive moments without sacrificing instrument separation. Overall, the performance is solid with standout improvisations that mark this early Brent era as a turning point in the band’s live evolution.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (glassberg audience and nak700) express clear positive sentiment, praising the explosive Eyes of the World, Not Fade Away, and overall high‑octane second set; one mixed recording notes decent playing but no criticism. No negative sentiment was found across analyses, so a modest +1.0 adjustment reflects consistent praise without unanimity.

best_recording.reason: Clear audience capture with good instrument separation; conveys the explosive second‑set energy and guest Cipollina moments while remaining listenable.

key_highlights: Explosive Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World → Not Fade Away jam marathon
Brent Mydland's fresh keyboard textures early in his tenure
Generous drum segment from Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann
Clear audience recording that preserves live energy

band_performance.Jerry: Scorching lead work throughout, especially alongside John Cipollina on Not Fade Away; vocal power strong and a jazzy solo on Candyman.
band_performance.Phil: Strong bass foundation with more extroverted vocal contributions; highlighted interplay with Brent on Peggy‑O.
band_performance.Brent: First month shines—delicate keys on Peggy‑O, energetic organ/synth textures, and enthusiastic second‑set contributions that lift the jam flow.
band_performance.Mickey: Generous drum segment praised; dynamic double‑drumming adds depth to the jam corridor.
band_performance.Billy: Provides solid rhythmic backbone alongside Mickey; part of the generous Rhythm Devils drumming highlighted in the show.
